lessonTitle: Curative Therapies - Bone Marrow/Stem Cell Transplant and Gene Therapy

items.html: Identify curative options in Sickle Cell Disease
Review steps for referring patients to the transplant team
Identify various conditioning regimens and their impact on the patient
Discuss how gene therapy works
Contrast and compare gene therapy options

data.html:
For patients with significantly impaired quality of life despite therapy, transplant or gene therapy (with all of the risks and benefits) may be worth it. First, let's look at what assessments need to be done by the Paediatrician/Provider before referring a patient to the transplant team.

Another consideration is the risk for graft vs host disease or rejection, which varies depending on if the donor is an exact match (HLA identical) or a partial match (HLA haploidentical).

Now that you have completed your assessments and confirmed you have a donor, let's look at a proposed algorithm for the referral of patients with SCD for transplant.

Brazauskas et. al. (2020) have looked at a risk score to predict event free survival after stem cell transplant. Take a look at each prognostic score (the lower the better) based on age and HLA matching. Prognostic scores: • Age <12 = 0 • Age >13 =1 • HLA Matched Sib =0 • HLA mismatched relative = 2 • MUD =1 • Mismatched unrelated donor= 2

To increase the likelihood for Human Leukocyte Antigen (HLA) matching between recipient and donor, ethnic background is important to consider. Since HLA markers are inherited, a potential recipient's best chance for matching is with someone of the same ethnic background.

slides.title: Conditioning Regimens Myeloablative vs Reduced Intensity

data.html:
Conditioning regimen: Busulfan + gene therapy (lentiviral) Resulted in MDS/Leukemia in 2 patients; halted for investigation

Goals: Adding functional globin (normal B or gamma for HbF), Inhibiting HbF inhibition (BCL11A) to net increase HbF Direct correction of SCD mutations Only on trial at this point

slides.title: Typical Process of Gene Therapy for Hemoglobinopathies

items.html: Erythrocytapheresis for > 2 months prior to mobilization
Plerixafor mobilization (no G-CSF for SCD)
Target > 2 x 10<sup>6</sup> CD34+ cells/kg
Busulfan full myeloablation
Transfection/electroporation and expansion
Approximately 10 weeks from mobilization to transplant
Replication defective
Self-inactivating (reduces insertional oncogenesis)
Vector packaging capacity (10 kb)
Semi-random integration
Variations in vector copy number (∝ transduction efficiency)

data.html: BCL11A suppresses γ-globin production, which makes HbF. γ-globin production can be reactivated by inactivating BCL11A (CRISPR-Cas9) or the BCL11A binding site at the globin promoter (CRISPR-Cas12a).

Let's take a look at the levels of hemoglobin fractionation (HbF) and other Hb adducts over time for Patient 1, who was treated with CRISPR-Cas9 for transfusion-dependent β-thalassemia (Panel A), and in Patient 2, who was treated with CRISPR-Cas9 for SCD (Panel D).

slides.title: Lovo-cel HGB-206 Group C and HGB-210
Exagamglogene Autotemcel for Severe Sickle Cell Disease

data.html:
Study Details

44 participants, 12 to 35 years of age with SCD and ≥ 2 severe VOCs per year in the previous 2 years. 30 participants followed for ≥ 16 months after exa-cel infusion were analysed.

  • 96.7% (29/30) had no severe VOCs for ≥ 12 consecutive months (VF12)
  • Mean VOC-free duration: 22.4 months
  • 100% of participants free from in-patient hospitalization for severe VOCs for ≥12 consecutive months

Safety profile is consistent with myeloablative Busulfan conditioning and autologous HSCT. No malignancies and no safety adverse events related to Exa-cel.

RUBY (NCT04853576) and EdiThal (NCT05444894) are Phase I/II, multi-center, open-label, single-arm studies evaluating the safety, efficacy, and tolerability of reni-cel in patients with severe SCD and transfusion dependent thalassemia. We will focus on SCD patients and RUBY.

• No Vaso-Occlusive events (VOEs) have been observed in patients with SCD since infusion with Reni-cel
  • Compared with a mean (SD) of 4.0 (1.4) severe VOEs/year in the 2 years before enrollment (n=10), all patients are completely VOE-free post-Reni-cel infusion
  • After receiving the last RBC transfusion at 0.5–2.2 months post Reni-cel infusion, all 5 patients have been transfusion-free for a range of 1.8–7.5 months

slides.title: Lived Experience Advocate: How Has Your Journey Changed?

data.html: Emmanuel is a Lived Experience Advocate who felt it was important to share where things are for him post transplant and what his experience was like.

data.title: Lived Experience Advocate: How Has Your Journey Changed?

items.html: Bone marrow transplant, gene therapy are the two curative options for individuals with Sickle Cell Disease
Children under 12 years of age with a matched related sibling donor, have the best 5 year event free survival rate
Exa-cel and Reni-cel are both showing great efficacy and promise for individuals with severe VOEs
